title: Initialize Git & Push Scaffold
description:
Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine. Branch: feature/devops-migration (already exists). Create the following new directories and placeholder files:

1. llm-service/ — new LLM microservice root
2. llm-service/app/ — application package
3. llm-service/app/agents/ — LLM agent modules (will be populated by refactorer)
4. backend/app/clients/ — HTTP client modules for inter-service calls
5. k8s/ — Kubernetes manifest directory
6. Create __init__.py files in llm-service/app/ and llm-service/app/agents/ and backend/app/clients/
7. Update shared/project_structure.json with all new directories and files.

Do NOT create Dockerfiles, K8s manifests, or source code — those are assigned to other agents.

title: Alembic migration 003: reconcile schema drift
description:
Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine. READ backend/alembic/versions/001_initial_schema.py and 002_add_question_mode.py for style.

CREATE backend/alembic/versions/003_reconcile_schema.py:
- revision = '003', down_revision = '002'
- upgrade():
  1. ADD COLUMN answer_flags TEXT NULL to responses table
  2. DROP COLUMN context_similarity_threshold from surveys table (use batch_alter_table for SQLite compat)
  3. DROP COLUMN question_embedding from responses table (use batch_alter_table for SQLite compat)
- downgrade():
  1. ADD COLUMN context_similarity_threshold REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.7 to surveys
  2. ADD COLUMN question_embedding TEXT NULL to responses
  3. DROP COLUMN answer_flags from responses

IMPORTANT: Use op.batch_alter_table() for all DROP COLUMN operations (SQLite does not support ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN directly). Match the existing migration style exactly.

title: PostgreSQL dual-dialect support in backend
description:
Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine. READ each file before editing.

1. EDIT backend/app/core/database.py:
   - Detect dialect from DATABASE_URL string (check if it starts with 'sqlite' or 'postgresql')
   - Only create engine with connect_args={'timeout': 30} for SQLite
   - For PostgreSQL: create engine without SQLite-specific connect_args
   - Only register the @event.listens_for PRAGMA listener when dialect is sqlite
   - Only call os.makedirs('data') in init_db() when dialect is sqlite
   - For PostgreSQL: no special event listeners needed

2. EDIT backend/app/core/config.py:
   - Keep DATABASE_URL default as sqlite+aiosqlite:///./data/cse.db (fallback for local dev)
   - Add new setting: LLM_SERVICE_URL: str = 'http://localhost:8001' (will be used later by llm_client)

3. EDIT backend/app/main.py:
   - Make os.makedirs('data') conditional: only when DATABASE_URL contains 'sqlite'

4. EDIT backend/pyproject.toml:
   - Add asyncpg>=0.30.0 to dependencies list

5. EDIT backend/alembic.ini:
   - Change sqlalchemy.url line to read from environment: use a comment explaining to set DATABASE_URL env var
   - Keep the sqlite URL as a commented fallback

6. EDIT backend/alembic/env.py:
   - Import os, read DATABASE_URL from os.environ with fallback
   - Override config sqlalchemy.url with the env value
   - Make render_as_batch conditional: only True when URL contains 'sqlite'
   - Keep async engine pattern

NOTE: Tests will continue using in-memory SQLite (sqlite+aiosqlite://) — do not break conftest.py.

title: Extract LLM service as separate FastAPI app
description:
Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine. This is a GREEN-BASELINE refactor — run 'cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -v' FIRST and confirm all tests pass before making changes.

Create the llm-service/ microservice (directories should already exist from task 1):

1. READ all files in backend/app/agents/ to understand current LLM logic:
   - generator_agent.py (generate_question, generate_preset_question_set)
   - validator.py (validate_with_llm, check_compound_question, check_leading_question)
   - answer_guardrails.py (check_answer_guardrails)
   - prompts.py (all prompt builders)

2. CREATE llm-service/pyproject.toml:
   - name: cse-llm-service
   - dependencies: fastapi, uvicorn, litellm, openai-agents, pydantic-settings, pydantic
   - python >= 3.11

3. CREATE llm-service/app/__init__.py (empty)
4. CREATE llm-service/app/agents/__init__.py (empty)

5. COPY (adapt) agent files to llm-service/app/agents/:
   - generator_agent.py, validator.py, answer_guardrails.py, prompts.py
   - Fix imports: change 'from app.agents.' to relative imports within the package
   - Remove any database/SQLAlchemy imports (LLM service is stateless)

6. CREATE llm-service/app/config.py:
   - Settings class with: GEMINI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY, GEMINI_MODEL, GEMINI_VALIDATOR_MODEL, GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS, LOG_LEVEL
   - Same effective_api_key property pattern as backend config

7. CREATE llm-service/app/schemas.py:
   - Pydantic models for request/response: GenerateQuestionRequest, GenerateQuestionResponse, ValidateQuestionRequest, ValidateQuestionResponse, CheckGuardrailsRequest, CheckGuardrailsResponse, GeneratePresetRequest, GeneratePresetResponse
   - Match the API contract in shared/plan.md section 7

8. CREATE llm-service/app/routes.py:
   - POST /generate-question: accepts survey context + history + question_number, calls generator_agent.generate_question(), returns question
   - POST /validate-question: calls validator functions
   - POST /check-guardrails: calls answer_guardrails.check_answer_guardrails()
   - POST /generate-preset-questions: calls generator_agent.generate_preset_question_set()
   - All endpoints have proper error handling (try/except with HTTPException)

9. CREATE llm-service/app/main.py:
   - FastAPI app with title 'CSE LLM Service'
   - Include routes router
   - CORS middleware (allow all origins for internal service)
   - GET /health endpoint returning status + model name
   - Logging configuration

Do NOT modify backend/ code in this task — that is task 5.

title: Refactor backend to call LLM service via HTTP
description:
Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine. IMPORTANT: Run backend tests FIRST to confirm green baseline.

1. CREATE backend/app/clients/llm_client.py:
   - Import httpx, asyncio
   - Import LLM_SERVICE_URL from app.core.config settings
   - Class LLMClient with async methods:
     a. generate_question(survey_data: dict, history: list, question_number: int) -> dict
        - POST to {LLM_SERVICE_URL}/generate-question
        - Return {question_text, question_id}
     b. validate_question(question: str, survey_context: str) -> dict
        - POST to {LLM_SERVICE_URL}/validate-question
        - Return {is_valid, issues}
     c. check_guardrails(answer: str, question: str) -> dict
        - POST to {LLM_SERVICE_URL}/check-guardrails
        - Return {is_valid, flags, rejection_reason}
     d. generate_preset_questions(survey_data: dict, count: int) -> list[dict]
        - POST to {LLM_SERVICE_URL}/generate-preset-questions
        - Return list of {question_number, question_id, text}
     e. health_check() -> dict
        - GET {LLM_SERVICE_URL}/health
   - Use httpx.AsyncClient with timeout=120s (LLM calls can be slow)
   - Retry logic: 3 attempts with exponential backoff for connection errors
   - All methods raise descriptive exceptions on failure
   - Module-level singleton: llm_client = LLMClient()

2. EDIT backend/app/services/question_service.py:
   - Replace 'from app.agents.generator_agent import generate_question' with 'from app.clients.llm_client import llm_client'
   - Update generate_next_question() dynamic path: call llm_client.generate_question() instead of direct agent call
   - Update validate calls: call llm_client.validate_question() instead of direct validator import
   - Adapt return value handling for HTTP response format

3. EDIT backend/app/services/survey_service.py:
   - Replace 'from app.agents.generator_agent import generate_preset_question_set' with 'from app.clients.llm_client import llm_client'
   - Update generate_preset_questions(): call llm_client.generate_preset_questions() instead

4. EDIT backend/app/services/session_service.py:
   - Replace 'from app.agents.answer_guardrails import check_answer_guardrails' with 'from app.clients.llm_client import llm_client'
   - Update answer submission: call llm_client.check_guardrails() instead of direct import

5. KEEP backend/app/agents/ directory intact (as fallback reference). Do NOT delete it.

6. EDIT backend/tests/conftest.py (if needed):
   - Add mock/fixture for llm_client that returns canned responses
   - Existing tests should NOT call the real LLM service

7. Run 'cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -v' and verify all tests still pass.

Write results to shared/learnings.md if any non-obvious issues arise.

title: Backend Dockerfile
description:
Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine. READ shared/plan.md for full context.

CREATE backend/Dockerfile:
- Multi-stage build
- Builder stage: python:3.11-slim, WORKDIR /app, copy pyproject.toml, pip install dependencies (including asyncpg which needs libpq-dev)
- Runtime stage: python:3.11-slim, copy from builder, copy app/ and alembic/ directories
- Expose port 8000
- Non-root user (appuser)
- HEALTHCHECK: curl -f http://localhost:8000/health || exit 1
- CMD: python -m uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000
- ENV: DATABASE_URL, LLM_SERVICE_URL, CORS_ORIGINS

CREATE backend/.dockerignore:
- __pycache__, *.pyc, .env, data/, tests/, .pytest_cache, .git

title: LLM service Dockerfile
description:
Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine. READ shared/plan.md for full context.

CREATE llm-service/Dockerfile:
- Multi-stage build, python:3.11-slim
- Builder stage: copy pyproject.toml, pip install deps
- Runtime stage: copy app/, expose port 8001
- Non-root user (appuser)
- HEALTHCHECK: curl -f http://localhost:8001/health || exit 1
- CMD: python -m uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8001
- ENV: GEMINI_API_KEY, GEMINI_MODEL, GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS
- Mount point for Google credentials: /app/credentials/

CREATE llm-service/.dockerignore:
- __pycache__, *.pyc, .env, .pytest_cache, .git

title: Frontend Dockerfile + nginx config
description:
Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine. READ frontend/vite.config.ts and frontend/package.json for build details.

CREATE frontend/Dockerfile:
- Multi-stage build
- Build stage: node:20-alpine, WORKDIR /app, copy package.json + package-lock.json, npm ci, copy src/, ARG VITE_API_BASE_URL=/api/v1, npm run build
- Runtime stage: nginx:alpine, copy --from=build /app/dist to /usr/share/nginx/html, copy nginx.conf to /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
- Expose port 80
- CMD: nginx -g 'daemon off;'

CREATE frontend/nginx.conf:
- Server block listening on port 80
- location / { root /usr/share/nginx/html; try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html; } (SPA routing)
- location /api/ { proxy_pass http://backend:8000; proxy_set_header Host $host; proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr; }
- Gzip compression for text/html, application/json, text/css, application/javascript

CREATE frontend/.dockerignore:
- node_modules, dist, .env, .git

title: Docker Compose configuration
description:
Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine. READ shared/plan.md for architecture.

CREATE docker-compose.yml at workspace root:
- version: '3.8'
- services:
  1. backend: build ./backend, ports 8000:8000, env_file .env, depends_on llm-service, environment DATABASE_URL + LLM_SERVICE_URL=http://llm-service:8001 + CORS_ORIGINS, healthcheck
  2. llm-service: build ./llm-service, ports 8001:8001, env_file .env, volumes for Google credentials, environment GEMINI_API_KEY + GEMINI_MODEL + GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS, healthcheck
  3. frontend: build ./frontend, ports 80:80, depends_on backend
- No database container (NeonDB is external)
- Networks: default bridge

CREATE docker-compose.dev.yml:
- Override for development: volume mounts for hot reload (backend/app:/app/app, llm-service/app:/app/app, frontend/src:/app/src), uvicorn --reload flag

UPDATE .env.example at workspace root:
- Add DATABASE_URL, LLM_SERVICE_URL, GEMINI_API_KEY, GEMINI_MODEL, GEMINI_VALIDATOR_MODEL, GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS, CORS_ORIGINS, LOG_LEVEL entries with descriptions

title: K8s manifests: namespace, config, deployments, services
description:
Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine. READ shared/plan.md for architecture.

CREATE k8s/ directory with these manifests:

1. k8s/namespace.yaml: Namespace 'cse'

2. k8s/configmap.yaml: ConfigMap 'cse-config' in cse namespace with:
   - CORS_ORIGINS=["*"]
   - LOG_LEVEL=info
   - LLM_SERVICE_URL=http://llm-service.cse.svc.cluster.local:8001

3. k8s/secrets.yaml: Secret 'cse-secrets' (TEMPLATE with base64 placeholders) with:
   - DATABASE_URL
   - GEMINI_API_KEY
   - GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS_JSON
   - Comment: replace placeholder values before applying

4. k8s/backend-deployment.yaml: Deployment 'backend' in cse namespace
   - 2 replicas, image cse-backend:latest
   - envFrom configmap + secret
   - readinessProbe: GET /health, liveness: GET /health
   - resources: requests 256Mi/250m, limits 512Mi/500m

5. k8s/backend-service.yaml: Service 'backend' ClusterIP port 8000

6. k8s/llm-service-deployment.yaml: Deployment 'llm-service' in cse namespace
   - 2 replicas, image cse-llm-service:latest
   - envFrom configmap + secret
   - readinessProbe: GET /health, liveness: GET /health
   - resources: requests 256Mi/250m, limits 1Gi/1000m (LLM processing)
   - Volume mount for Google credentials if using service account JSON

7. k8s/llm-service-service.yaml: Service 'llm-service' ClusterIP port 8001

8. k8s/frontend-deployment.yaml: Deployment 'frontend' in cse namespace
   - 2 replicas, image cse-frontend:latest
   - readinessProbe: GET / port 80, liveness: GET / port 80
   - resources: requests 64Mi/100m, limits 128Mi/200m

9. k8s/frontend-service.yaml: Service 'frontend' LoadBalancer port 80

All manifests must use consistent labels: app.kubernetes.io/name, app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cse

title: K8s Alembic migration job
description:
Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine.

CREATE k8s/migration-job.yaml:
- Kind: Job, name 'alembic-migrate', namespace cse
- Uses backend image (cse-backend:latest)
- Command: python -m alembic upgrade head
- envFrom: cse-secrets (needs DATABASE_URL)
- restartPolicy: Never
- backoffLimit: 3
- ttlSecondsAfterFinished: 300 (auto-cleanup after 5 min)
- Annotations: helm.sh/hook: pre-install,pre-upgrade (if using Helm later)

title: Deployment script deploy.ps1
description:
Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine.

CREATE deploy.ps1 at workspace root:
- PowerShell script for K8s deployment
- Parameters: -Registry (container registry URL), -Tag (image tag, default 'latest'), -DryRun (switch)
- Steps:
  1. Build Docker images: docker build -t {registry}/cse-backend:{tag} ./backend, same for llm-service, frontend
  2. Push images: docker push (skip if DryRun)
  3. Apply K8s manifests in order:
     a. kubectl apply -f k8s/namespace.yaml
     b. kubectl apply -f k8s/secrets.yaml (warn: ensure secrets are populated)
     c. kubectl apply -f k8s/configmap.yaml
     d. kubectl apply -f k8s/migration-job.yaml — kubectl wait --for=condition=complete job/alembic-migrate -n cse --timeout=120s
     e. kubectl apply -f k8s/backend-deployment.yaml k8s/backend-service.yaml
     f. kubectl apply -f k8s/llm-service-deployment.yaml k8s/llm-service-service.yaml
     g. kubectl apply -f k8s/frontend-deployment.yaml k8s/frontend-service.yaml
  4. Verify rollout: kubectl rollout status deployment/backend -n cse --timeout=120s (same for llm-service, frontend)
  5. Print service endpoints
- DryRun mode: print commands without executing
- Error handling: stop on first failure with clear error message

title: Backend code review
description:
Review ALL backend code changes from tasks 2, 3, 4, 5:

1. Migration 003: verify upgrade/downgrade are correct, batch_alter_table usage
2. database.py: dialect detection logic, connection pooling, no SQLite leaks for PG
3. config.py: LLM_SERVICE_URL setting, env defaults
4. main.py: conditional data dir creation
5. alembic.ini + env.py: env-based URL, conditional batch mode
6. llm-service/: proper FastAPI structure, error handling, agent logic preserved correctly
7. llm_client.py: timeout handling, retry logic, error propagation
8. Service layer changes: correct HTTP call adaptation, response parsing
9. Security: no secrets in code, proper error messages (no internal details leaked)
10. Test preservation: existing tests still pass with mocked llm_client

Write review findings to shared/learnings.md.

title: Architecture review
description:
Review overall architecture post-refactor:

1. Microservice boundaries: is the LLM service boundary correct? Any circular dependencies?
2. Inter-service communication: HTTP client pattern, error handling, timeout strategy
3. Database access: only backend accesses DB, LLM service is stateless — verify
4. K8s topology: service discovery, resource limits, health checks, scaling strategy
5. Docker build: multi-stage builds, image sizes, security (non-root, no secrets baked in)
6. Configuration management: env vars flow correctly through docker-compose and K8s
7. Migration strategy: Alembic job runs before deployments
8. Frontend: nginx proxy config matches backend service name
9. Failure modes: what happens if LLM service is down? Does backend degrade gracefully?

Write review findings to shared/learnings.md.

title: E2E test verification
description:
Run E2E tests against the refactored multi-service system. Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine.

Pre-requisites: both backend (port 8000) and llm-service (port 8001) must be running.

Verify:
1. All 28 existing E2E scenarios from tests/e2e/test_full_flow.py still pass
2. Health endpoints: GET /health on backend (8000) and llm-service (8001)
3. Admin creates survey (backend -> DB)
4. Participant starts session and answers questions (backend -> llm-service -> backend -> DB)
5. Preset question generation works (backend -> llm-service)
6. Answer guardrails work (backend -> llm-service)
7. Survey completion flow end-to-end

Write results to shared/e2e_scenarios.json.

title: Update documentation
description:
Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine. READ existing docs/ first.

UPDATE docs/architecture.md:
- New microservice architecture diagram (backend + llm-service + frontend)
- Service communication patterns
- Database: NeonDB PostgreSQL

UPDATE docs/setup.md:
- Docker Compose local development instructions
- Environment variables reference (DATABASE_URL, LLM_SERVICE_URL, etc.)
- How to run without Docker (traditional way)

CREATE docs/deployment.md:
- K8s deployment guide
- Prerequisites: kubectl, Docker, container registry
- Step-by-step: build images, configure secrets, deploy
- deploy.ps1 usage
- Monitoring: health check endpoints, logs

UPDATE README.md:
- Update architecture section
- Add Docker quickstart section
- Add K8s deployment section
- Update tech stack to include PostgreSQL, Docker, K8s

title: Git commit & push all changes
description:
Workspace root: d:\GitHub\conversational-survey-engine. Branch: feature/devops-migration.

Commit all changes with descriptive commit messages:
1. Stage all new and modified files
2. Commit with message: 'feat(devops): migrate to PostgreSQL, extract LLM microservice, add Docker/K8s deployment'
3. Push to remote origin feature/devops-migration

Verify: git status shows clean working tree after push.
